content:
Shared components should simplify your architecture — not become a dumping ground for every reusable idea. Learn how enterprise Angular teams decide what truly belongs in shared/, avoid "God Components," and build scalable applications with clear ownership and maintainable UI.

5 min read
5 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Shared Folder Starts With Good Intentions
Every Angular project begins with the same goal: build reusable UI.
You create a ButtonComponent.
Then an InputComponent.
A ModalComponent.
Maybe a SpinnerComponent.
Everything feels clean, organized, and reusable.
The shared/ folder quickly becomes one of your favorite places in the project.
Then the application starts growing.
The Customer feature needs a profile card.
The Orders feature needs a summary card.
The Reports feature needs a statistics widget.
